commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Data now survives pod restarts and redeployments.

commit.message:
fix: replace Unicode symbol icons with SVGs (#375) (#383)

* fix: replace Unicode symbol icons with SVGs to prevent tofu squares (#375)

Unicode characters like ✎ ✗ ☰ − render as blank squares on devices
without those glyphs in their font. Replace with SVG icons from
qrc:/icons/ which render identically on all platforms.

Changes:
- ProfileSelectorPage: Edit/Delete/Remove menu items use SVG icons
- ShotHistoryPage: Saved button uses list.svg icon
- Added minus.svg and trash.svg to icon set
- CLAUDE.md: Added rules against Unicode symbols as icons and
  overriding FINAL properties on Qt types

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: replace all risky Unicode symbols with SVG icons across app

Comprehensive replacement of Unicode characters that render as tofu
squares on devices without the right font glyphs:

- ✓ checkmarks → tick.svg (15 instances across 12 files)
- ✕ X marks → cross.svg (6 instances across 4 files)
- ★☆ stars → star.svg/star-outline.svg (6 instances)
- ⋮ vertical ellipsis → more-vertical.svg (1 instance)
- ⚠ warning → warning.svg (1 instance)
- ≡ hamburger → list.svg (1 instance)

Added warning.svg and more-vertical.svg to icon set.

Safe Unicode characters (°, ·, —, →, ×, ▼, ▲) left unchanged as they
have near-universal font support.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add CLAUDE.md rules for Unicode symbols and FINAL properties

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: generate AndroidManifest.xml from template at build time

AndroidManifest.xml was checked in with hardcoded versionCode and
versionName that would drift from CMakeLists.txt and versioncode.txt,
causing a perpetually dirty working tree after builds.

Now generated from AndroidManifest.xml.in by ApplyVersionCode.cmake,
matching the existing pattern for version.h and version.iss. Both
versionCode and versionName come from their single source of truth.

Changes:
- Added android/AndroidManifest.xml.in template with @placeholders@
- Updated ApplyVersionCode.cmake to use configure_file()
- Removed manifest sed from Android CI (only versioncode.txt committed)
- Gitignored the generated AndroidManifest.xml
- Updated CLAUDE.md versioning docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: make MAX_POINTS public to fix build error

Moving MAX_POINTS from private to public in FastLineRenderer so it can
be accessed from file scope in fastlinerenderer.cpp (needed after PR #381
replaced the duplicate STRIP_MAX_POINTS constant).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add cmake warning for missing manifest template + restore color-coded import icons

- ApplyVersionCode.cmake now warns when AndroidManifest.xml.in is missing
  instead of silently skipping manifest generation
- ProfileImportPage status icons now use colored circle backgrounds
  (primary/success/warning) to preserve semantic distinction lost when
  switching from colored Unicode text to white SVGs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: restore original icon colors lost in Unicode→SVG migration

Use Button with icon.source + icon.color to tint white SVGs to their
original semantic colors (primaryColor, successColor, warningButtonColor).
Affects dropdown checkmarks, completion overlay, import status icons,
success badges, and sort picker checkmarks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add ColoredIcon component and use theme colors for all SVG icons

Create ColoredIcon.qml — a display-only tinted SVG icon that uses
Button's internal IconLabel for icon.color tinting. Replace all
opacity-hack and raw-Button workarounds with ColoredIcon.

This ensures icons respect user-customizable theme colors
(textSecondaryColor, primaryColor, successColor, etc.) instead of
using hardcoded white with opacity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: add icon.source support to AccessibleButton

Update AccessibleButton contentItem to handle both icon+text and
text-only rendering via a Row with Image + Text. This eliminates
verbose contentItem overrides at call sites.

Simplify 3 ShotHistoryPage buttons from ~15-line contentItem
overrides to single icon.source property.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: ColoredIcon opacity, search clear button, and i18n

- ColoredIcon: remove enabled:false which caused Material style to
  render all icons at 38% opacity. Use absorbing MouseArea instead.
- ShotHistoryPage: change search clear from Text to Item with explicit
  size so the icon and MouseArea are visible/functional.
- VisualizerMultiImportPage: wrap 2 hardcoded English legend strings
  with TranslationManager.translate().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
✨ FEATURE: Add Date Range Filtering to v1.6.0

🆕 New Date Filter Options:
- date_range="recent" - Show press releases from last 3 months
- date_range="last_month" - Show press releases from last month
- date_range="last_year" - Show press releases from last year
- date_range="2024-01-01,2024-12-31" - Custom date ranges

💡 Usage Examples:
- [press_releases date_range="recent"] - Recent releases only
- [press_releases date_range="recent" limit="5"] - Last 5 recent releases
- [press_releases date_range="2024-06-01,2024-12-31"] - Custom range

⚡ Benefits:
- Faster page loading with fewer posts
- Better user experience with current content
- SEO benefits from fresh content signals
- Full backward compatibility maintained

🔧 Technical Implementation:
- Uses WordPress date_query for optimal performance
- Database-level filtering for efficiency
- Respects WordPress timezone settings
- No breaking changes to existing shortcodes

🎯 Perfect for:
- Homepage recent news sections
- Archive pages by date
- Quarterly/seasonal content display
- Mobile-friendly recent releases

All existing shortcodes continue to work unchanged!

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: first-run.sh always passes --confirm to sub-scripts

first-run.sh owns its own interactive gating via confirm() per step.
Sub-scripts should not double-prompt — they now always receive --confirm
when called from first-run.sh, matching the linux-security bootstrap pattern.

commit.message:
Kill captain, replace with hub + overseer architecture (#62)

* Kill captain, replace with hub + overseer architecture

Captain agent was in the critical path between voice commands and worker
execution, adding latency and fragility. This restructures the system:

- Rename voice-server → hub: becomes the control plane (creates workers,
  routes voice, manages projects via new worker-manager.js)
- Rename captain → overseer: lightweight monitor that only watches workers,
  nudges lazy ones, and speaks updates (defaults to codex)
- Remove Terminal tab, revamp Projects tab with tiled panes, per-pane input,
  worker creation UI, zoom/fullscreen, idle indicators
- Voice requires explicit pane focus (no smart routing)
- Two-tier idle detection: 30s → overseer pinged, 60s → human pinged via speak
- Hub handles worker CRUD via /api/workers endpoints
- Overseer stripped of create-worker and create_pending_task capabilities

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix test Dockerfile: remove deleted create-worker, add worker-manager.js

The create-worker shell script was removed from the overseer (worker
creation moved to hub's worker-manager.js). Update the test Dockerfile
and worker-dialog spec to reference the new location.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update tests for new hub + overseer UI structure

- Remove Overseer tab tests for removed terminal/controls elements
- Update element selectors (#mic-btn → #voice-mic-btn, etc.)
- text_command test now expects deprecation error
- Update heartbeat test for new message wording
- Fix integration tests to use pane_send_text instead of text_command
- Update screenshots test for new tab structure
- Fix projects test for 4-tab layout (was 5)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Rename captain.spec.js → overseer.spec.js

Addresses Copilot review comment about inconsistent naming between
the TEST_OVERSEER env var and the old captain.spec.js filename.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix remaining test failures: voice tab navigation and STT error display

- autoread test: navigate to voice tab before checking toggle
- voice-history-modal: expand <details> panel before clicking #voice-summary
- showSttError: display actual error message in voiceTranscriptionEl
  (was showing generic "Error" instead of "timed out" message)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix autoread and STT tests: play count reset, show actual messages

- autoread: Reset __playCount after voice tab click (unlockAudio()
  triggers ttsAudio.play() on user gesture, inflating the count)
- app.js: Show actual transcription/error text in voiceTranscriptionEl
  instead of generic "Sent"/"Error" placeholders

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

fix: disable auth guard on interactions so editor works without login

infra: migrate k8s manifests + setup script from minikube to k3s

- k8s manifests: image refs → localhost:5000/agentnet/* with
  imagePullPolicy: Always (k3s pulls from local registry, no more
  imagePullPolicy: Never minikube hack)

commit.message:
chore: add .claude/ to gitignore

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add useEffect ref syncs to prevent stale refs in keyboard handler

Root cause: 30+ places call setClips/setCurrentTime/setSelectedClipIds
directly (not through synced wrappers), causing clipsRef, currentTimeRef,
and selectedClipIdsRef to become stale. When Q/W/M shortcuts read these
refs, they see old values and can't find clips at the current position.

Fix: add useEffect syncs for clips, currentTime, selectedClipIds so refs
stay up-to-date regardless of which code path mutates the state.

Also adds debug console.log for Q/W/M shortcuts to verify handler fires
and show actual ref values (check browser DevTools Console).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: replace executable/context_json on Execution with Context model

Introduce Orkestr::Context model with JSONB data and contextualizable
polymorphic association. Execution now references Context instead of
storing context_json directly and having an executable polymorphic.
Add Contextualizable concern for host app models.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

→ Effect<void, InputReadError, R>.

eval: NanoClaw — container-isolated personal AI agent (watch)

vs dossier execution), and fork maintenance burden outweigh
benefits for solopreneur setup. Revisit when /loop proves
insufficient or API pricing improves.

- Regex: fraction alternation order (1/2 parsed as 1), spaced fractions
- Regex: add Strategy 4.5 (N viên mỗi ngày) and Strategy 5 (uống N gói)
- Legacy calc: return None for liquid+ml without volume, mg unit mismatch
- Tests: fix mock patch target (calculator namespace), remove bad assertion
- PRN exclusion for Strategy 5 (sốt/đau/khi cần)

New features:
- MCP web-search client (Node.js multi-engine: DDG, Google, Bing)

feat: add --confirm safety gate and --help to all scripts

Service-impacting scripts (harden-sshd, enable-stealth-firewall,
apply-defaults, apply-configs, deploy, brew-upgrade) now show a warning
block describing exactly what will change and prompt for AGREE before
proceeding. Pass --confirm to skip the prompt for scripted/automated use.

--auto in first-run.sh sets CONFIRM=true, propagating --confirm to
sub-scripts. --dry-run always bypasses the gate.

--help added to all 9 scripts with description, usage, and full flag list.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove Web Push notification system

PWA notifications aren't dependable without a persistent server —
browser throttles background service worker timers. Removing all
VAPID keys, push subscription endpoints, and client-side push code.

Auto-generate spark title on creation

the first sentence as title (up to 120 chars), falling back to first 80
chars. Applied in both the DB mutation and optimistic update.

commit.message:
fix: rename Dialog 'message' property to avoid Qt 6.10 FINAL conflict (#376) (#382)

Qt 6.10 marks Popup.message as FINAL, which prevents Dialog subclasses
from declaring their own 'message' property. This caused the Settings
History tab (and potentially Debug and Language tabs) to fail to load
entirely, appearing blank.

Renamed to 'resultMessage' in all 4 affected Dialog instances.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
